title: Forcepoint ONE CASB Shadow IT Application Discovery (`forcepoint:one`)
dataSources: Forcepoint cloud SSE (`forcepoint:one`), on-prem Web Security (`websense:cg:kv`), Email (`forcepoint:email:kv`), DLP (`forcepoint:dlp`)
app: Forcepoint Insights SIEM App (Splunkbase 8053); Forcepoint Web Security TA (`websense:cg:kv`); Email Security KV (`forcepoint:email:kv`)
spl:
index=sase sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(event_type),"(?i)casb|cloud app|saas")
| stats dc(app_name) as apps values(app_name) as examples by user
| where apps>12
| sort -apps
description: Unsanctioned SaaS spreads via credit-card signups; CASB logs reveal the real portfolio of cloud usage.
value: IT governance and security teams discover unsanctioned cloud applications used across the organization, quantify data exposure risk by upload volume and app risk score, and prioritize apps for blocking or formal sanctioning.
implementation: Install the Insights SIEM app, configure HEC inputs per product line, and align indexes (`proxy`, `email`, `dlp`, `sase`). Normalize usernames to enterprise SAMAccountName.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Email.All_Email
  by Email.user, Email.action, Email.src, Email.dest span=1h
| sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Forcepoint Web Security

knownFalsePositives: Spikes from new SaaS rollouts, automated workflows, sanctioned bulk uploads, or M&A integration.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Forcepoint Insights SIEM App (Splunkbase 8053) v1.x on the Search Head, or direct syslog/API integration from Forcepoint ONE SSE.
• Data in `index=sase` with `sourcetype=forcepoint:one`. Key fields: `event_type` (casb/web/ztna/dlp), `app_name`, `app_category`, `app_risk_score`, `user`, `action`, `bytes_in`, `bytes_out`, `src_ip`, `sanctioned` (yes/no/unknown).
• Forcepoint ONE SSE with CASB module enabled. The CASB module discovers cloud applications by analyzing web traffic through the SSE proxy or inline deployment.
• Create a `fp_sanctioned_apps.csv` lookup: `app_name,sanctioned,owner_team,approved_date` for marking sanctioned versus unsanctioned applications.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
In Forcepoint ONE admin portal: Settings > SIEM Integration > configure Splunk HEC or syslog endpoint. Enable CASB event logging.

Verify:
```spl
index=sase sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(event_type), "casb|cloud")
| stats count dc(app_name) as unique_apps by app_category
| sort - count
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Shadow IT discovery:
```spl
index=sase sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-7d
| where match(lower(event_type), "casb|cloud")
| stats count as sessions dc(user) as users sum(bytes_out) as upload_bytes by app_name, app_category, app_risk_score
| lookup fp_sanctioned_apps.csv app_name OUTPUT sanctioned owner_team
| where sanctioned!="yes" OR isnull(sanctioned)
| eval upload_mb=round(upload_bytes/1048576, 1)
| eval risk=case(app_risk_score>=8, "HIGH", app_risk_score>=5, "MEDIUM", 1==1, "LOW")
| sort - risk, - upload_mb
```

Understanding this SPL: Identifies cloud applications not on the sanctioned list, ranked by risk score and upload volume. High-risk unsanctioned apps with significant upload volume represent potential data exfiltration channels. Common shadow IT: personal cloud storage (Google Drive personal, Dropbox), code repositories (personal GitHub), collaboration tools (WhatsApp Web), and AI services (ChatGPT, Claude). Upload volume is more concerning than download — uploading to an unsanctioned app means company data is leaving controlled environments.

New app detection:
```spl
index=sase sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(event_type), "casb|cloud")
| stats count dc(user) as users by app_name
| join type=left app_name [
    search index=sase sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-31d latest=-1d
    | where match(lower(event_type), "casb|cloud")
    | stats count as hist_count by app_name
]
| where isnull(hist_count)
| sort - count
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) In Forcepoint ONE portal: CASB > Cloud App Discovery. Compare the discovered app list with Splunk.
(b) Access a known unsanctioned cloud app from a monitored endpoint. Verify it appears in Splunk.
(c) Verify the `fp_sanctioned_apps.csv` lookup is complete — every sanctioned app should be marked.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard ("Forcepoint — Shadow IT Discovery"):
• Row 1 — Single-value: "Unsanctioned apps detected", "High-risk apps", "Data uploaded to shadow IT (MB)".
• Row 2 — Table: app name, category, risk score, users, sessions, upload volume.
• Row 3 — Timechart: shadow IT usage over 30 days.

Alert: Weekly shadow IT report to IT governance. Immediate alert for any high-risk (score >= 8) unsanctioned app with upload volume > 100 MB.

Runbook:
1. **High-risk app with uploads** — Block the app in Forcepoint ONE policy. Investigate which users uploaded data and what was uploaded.
2. **Popular unsanctioned app (many users)** — The organization may need this app. Evaluate for sanctioning or provide a sanctioned alternative.
3. **AI/ML services detected** — Sensitive data may be shared with AI providers. Apply DLP inspection to the CASB channel for these apps.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Very few apps discovered** — CASB discovery requires traffic to pass through the Forcepoint proxy. If users bypass the proxy (split tunnel VPN), apps are not visible.
• **`app_name` shows raw hostnames instead of app names** — The Forcepoint app classification database may not recognize the hostname. Submit for classification.
• **Sanctioned apps appearing as unsanctioned** — Update the `fp_sanctioned_apps.csv` lookup with all approved applications.
grandmaExplanation: We find out which unapproved cloud services our employees are using, like personal file-sharing or AI tools, so we can make sure company data is not accidentally leaking out.
